recipeInstructions.text: In 2dl lukewarm milk, with 3 teaspoons of sugar and a little flour, let the yeast rise.
Knead the rest of the flour with the oil, the remaining sugar dissolved in lukewarm milk, a pinch of salt, and the whipped egg whites.
Add the risen yeast mixture, knead the dough, and knead until it is smooth and elastic.
Let it rise in a warm place until doubled in size.
Meanwhile, for the filling, mix the sugar with the pudding powder and cocoa, add the milk, and cook the pudding as usual.
Once ready, remove from heat and stir in the margarine cut into pieces.
Divide the risen dough into 6 equal balls, roll them out individually on a floured board, spread with pudding, and roll them up.
Twist the rolls together in pairs. This will make 3 loaves.
Place them in a greased baking pan (I baked them separately because they are large), far apart from each other, as they grow a lot.
Brush the tops with egg yolk, bake in a preheated oven for about 40 minutes until done.

x-recipe-keeper.name: Pudingos kalács
recipeDirections: In 2dl lukewarm milk, with 3 teaspoons of sugar and a little flour, let the yeast rise.
Knead the rest of the flour with the oil, the remaining sugar dissolved in lukewarm milk, a pinch of salt, and the whipped egg whites.
Add the risen yeast mixture, knead the dough, and knead until it is smooth and elastic.
Let it rise in a warm place until doubled in size.
Meanwhile, for the filling, mix the sugar with the pudding powder and cocoa, add the milk, and cook the pudding as usual.
Once ready, remove from heat and stir in the margarine cut into pieces.
Divide the risen dough into 6 equal balls, roll them out individually on a floured board, spread with pudding, and roll them up.
Twist the rolls together in pairs. This will make 3 loaves.
Place them in a greased baking pan (I baked them separately because they are large), far apart from each other, as they grow a lot.
Brush the tops with egg yolk, bake in a preheated oven for about 40 minutes until done.
